an2.77:1.1: “With a *nimitta*, *bhikkhus*, bad, unwholesome *dhammas* arise — not without a *nimitta*.
an2.77:1.2: By abandoning that very *nimitta*, those bad, unwholesome *dhammas* do not come to be.”
an2.78:1.1: “With a source, *bhikkhus*, bad, unwholesome *dhammas* arise — not without a source.
an2.78:1.2: By abandoning that very source, those bad, unwholesome *dhammas* do not come to be.”
an2.79:1.1: “With a cause, *bhikkhus*, bad, unwholesome *dhammas* arise — not without a cause.
an2.79:1.2: By abandoning that very cause, those bad, unwholesome *dhammas* do not come to be.”
an2.80:1.2: By abandoning those very *saṅkhāras*, those bad, unwholesome *dhammas* do not come to be.”
an2.81:1.1: “With a condition, *bhikkhus*, bad, unwholesome *dhammas* arise — not without a condition.
an2.81:1.2: By abandoning that very condition, those bad, unwholesome *dhammas* do not come to be.”
an2.82:1.1: “With form, *bhikkhus*, bad, unwholesome *dhammas* arise — not without form.
an2.82:1.2: By abandoning that very form, those bad, unwholesome *dhammas* do not come to be.”
an2.83:1.2: By abandoning that very *vedanā*, those bad, unwholesome *dhammas* do not come to be.”
an2.84:1.2: By abandoning that very *saññā*, those bad, unwholesome *dhammas* do not come to be.”
an2.85:1.1: “With consciousness [*viññāṇa*], *bhikkhus*, bad, unwholesome *dhammas* arise — not without consciousness [*viññāṇa*].
an2.85:1.2: By abandoning that very consciousness [*viññāṇa*], those bad, unwholesome *dhammas* do not come to be.”
an2.86:1.1: “With a *saṅkhata* object, *bhikkhus*, bad, unwholesome *dhammas* arise — not with an *asaṅkhata* object.
an2.86:1.2: By abandoning that very *saṅkhata*, those bad, unwholesome *dhammas* do not come to be.”
